mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% in 60%+ of instances, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with increasing consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to produce positive returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive when INDPRO declines and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-INDPRO interaction fails to generate positive returns in 50%+ of cases, or the joint effect's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 7.5% signals improving labor market conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when UNRATE falls below 7.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) signals aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to produce negative returns in 50%+ of cases, or the joint effect's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.15).
